title: Consolidate REDEPLOY.md from-scratch runbook
description: Aggregate docs/01..10 into a single docs/REDEPLOY.md that takes the user from empty Proxmox to self-hosted Supabase serving apps without Claude. Numbered, copy-pasteable, with rollback notes per step. Contractual deliverable for the project.
acceptance_criteria: - A second person (or future-you) following docs/REDEPLOY.md from scratch reaches a working stack. - All commands are copy-paste, not narrative. - Includes a common pitfalls section harvested from issues hit during the actual build.

title: Backup and restore runbook
description: Cron-driven pg_dump on the VM writing to /var/backups/supabase/ with retention (7 daily, 4 weekly). Snapshot storage volumes likewise. Test restore-from-backup end-to-end.
acceptance_criteria: - Backup cron in place, files appearing on schedule. - One full restore tested end-to-end. - docs/10_backup_restore.md is the source of truth for both directions.

title: Document live-clone path via logical replication (optional)
description: Investigate Postgres logical replication from cloud Supabase as publisher to self-hosted as subscriber. Requires wal_level=logical on cloud (Supabase supports this), publication on cloud side, subscription on self-hosted side. Document trade-offs (cloud egress, replication lag, conflict on schema drift) and runbook to enable/disable on demand. Do not enable by default.
acceptance_criteria: - docs/09_logical_replication.md captures the option, prerequisites, exact SQL, and how to fall back to one-shot if it breaks. - Decision recorded (enable now / defer / not pursuing).

title: Wire platform-portal/.env to new instance and verify with one app
description: Update platform-portal/.env with NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL=http://10.0.0.85:8000 and NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=<new>. Rebuild one standalone app (e.g. pss-matl-cert) so build-time NEXT_PUBLIC vars are baked in. Deploy and verify it boots, authenticates, reads/writes against the new DB.
acceptance_criteria: - platform-portal/.env updated. - pss-matl-cert (or chosen app) rebuilt with --build-arg pointing at new instance, deployed via existing GHCR/compose flow. - App login, list view, create/edit operation all succeed against self-hosted Supabase. - docs/08_app_integration.md describes the env edit + rebuild + deploy sequence.

title: Sync storage buckets from cloud to self-hosted
description: Use Supabase CLI (supabase storage ls/cp) or rclone against the project S3-compatible storage to download all bucket contents, then upload into self-hosted storage (writes to local disk under Docker volumes). Document re-sync procedure for periodic refresh.
acceptance_criteria: - All buckets present in self-hosted Studio. - Sample object from each bucket downloads via the new endpoint with the new anon/service key. - docs/07_storage_sync.md includes both initial sync and re-sync commands.

title: Migrate schema + data from cloud Supabase (one-shot)
description: Use pg_dump against cloud project direct DB connection (not pooler) to dump (a) public schema definitions, (b) public schema data, (c) auth.users rows only (not auth schema), (d) any custom schemas in use. Restore into self-hosted DB. Skip Supabase-managed schemas (auth, storage, realtime, supabase_functions, pgsodium, vault) — those are recreated by the stack. Verify row counts on representative tables.
acceptance_criteria: - public schema present with all tables, RLS policies, functions. - auth.users rows imported; existing users can sign in. - Row counts on top 5 largest tables match cloud +/- expected drift. - docs/06_data_migration.md gives the exact pg_dump and pg_restore commands and gotchas (extensions, sequences, owner remapping).

title: Bring up Supabase self-hosted stack
description: Clone supabase/supabase repo, copy docker/.env to /opt/pss-supabase-host/.env, set secrets from previous bead, docker compose up -d, wait for health. Smoke test: Studio at http://10.0.0.85:3000, Kong at http://10.0.0.85:8000/rest/v1/ returns auth error (expected).
acceptance_criteria: - All Supabase containers (db, auth, rest, realtime, storage, kong, studio, meta, imgproxy, vector) report healthy. - Studio accessible from LAN with dashboard creds. - Kong returns 401 for unauthenticated REST call. - docs/05_supabase_stack.md covers compose layout, key services and their ports, how to tail logs, how to restart cleanly.

title: Generate Supabase self-hosted secrets
description: Generate JWT_SECRET (>=32 chars), derive ANON_KEY and SERVICE_ROLE_KEY from it, set POSTGRES_PASSWORD, DASHBOARD_USERNAME and DASHBOARD_PASSWORD, SMTP creds (or skip mail for now). Store canonical .env.example in this repo with placeholder values; real .env never committed.
acceptance_criteria: - New JWT secret + derived keys generated (NOT reused from cloud project). - .env.example present in repo with every variable documented. - Real .env stored on the VM only, mode 600. - docs/04_secrets.md explains how to rotate JWT and why that forces an app rebuild.

title: Install Docker Engine + compose plugin
description: Install Docker Engine and docker compose v2 plugin from Docker official apt repo (not Ubuntu). Add deploy user to docker group. Verify with hello-world container and docker compose version.
acceptance_criteria: - docker --version and docker compose version both report current stable. - Non-root user can run docker without sudo. - docs/03_docker_install.md lists exact apt commands and signing-key setup.

title: Bootstrap OS hardening on Supabase VM
description: On the new VM: create non-root sudo user, lock root SSH login, install fail2ban, configure UFW (allow 22/SSH from LAN, 8000/Kong from LAN, 5432/Postgres from LAN-dev only), set timezone, enable unattended-upgrades for security patches.
acceptance_criteria: - Root SSH disabled, key-only auth enforced. - UFW active with documented rule set. - Unattended security upgrades enabled. - docs/02_os_bootstrap.md captures every command run, idempotently.

title: Provision Ubuntu 24.04 LTS VM on Proxmox
description: Create VM on Proxmox 10.0.0.84 from Ubuntu 24.04 cloud-init template. 4 vCPU / 8 GB RAM / 50 GB qcow2 thin disk. Static IP 10.0.0.85, DHCP reservation or netplan static. SSH key from dev workstation injected via cloud-init.
acceptance_criteria: - VM boots, reachable via SSH as the deploy user. - docs/01_proxmox_vm.md describes the cloud-init template, qm create flags or GUI clicks, network config, and how to recreate the VM from scratch. - VM resources visible in Proxmox dashboard match spec.
notes:
Doc drafted at docs/01_proxmox_vm.md. Two paths (CLI canonical, GUI fallback). Bead stays in_progress until user runs the steps and the verification checklist passes. Acceptance criteria mapped to a checklist at the end of the doc.
Hit '400 not enough arguments' from qm create in section A.1 — root cause: user pasted lines 56-62 (the qm create block on its own) rather than the whole heredoc, so ${TEMPLATE_VMID} was unset in their shell. Doc updated: qm create now splits across independent qm/qm-set lines (no backslash continuations), added a paste-as-one callout above the section, and a troubleshooting entry mapping the exact error to the recovery.
close_reason: VM provisioned and verified end-to-end. Smoke test output: hostname=pss-supabase-host; cores=4; ram=7.8Gi; disk=48G; ip=10.0.0.85. SSH key auth working, static IP correct, all six acceptance checks pass. Doc 01_proxmox_vm.md hardened along the way: split multi-line qm create into independent qm/qm-set lines, added paste-as-one callout, added '400 not enough arguments' troubleshooting entry, clarified the SSH-key prerequisite.

title: Stand up self-hosted Supabase dev instance on Proxmox
description:
Provision a Proxmox VM hosting a self-hosted Supabase instance that mirrors the existing cloud Supabase project, serving as the dev datastore for PSS standalone apps (per docs/SKILL_pss_standalone_app.md). All steps must be documented in this repo so the user can redeploy it themselves without Claude.

Target: Proxmox at 10.0.0.84
VM IP: 10.0.0.85 (proposed)
OS: Ubuntu Server 24.04 LTS
Specs: 4 vCPU / 8 GB RAM / 50 GB disk
Migration: pg_dump/restore one-shot for parity (Phase 1); logical replication as optional follow-up (Phase 2)
Integration: NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL=http://10.0.0.85:8000 in platform-portal/.env
acceptance_criteria:
- Self-hosted Supabase reachable from PSS apps on the LAN
- Schema and data parity with cloud (verified by row counts on representative tables)
- Storage buckets synced
- One PSS standalone app boots and operates normally against the new instance
- docs/REDEPLOY.md describes a from-zero rebuild that the user can follow without assistance
- Backup/restore runbook tested at least once

value: Project goal: stand up a self-hosted Supabase dev instance on Proxmox (10.0.0.84), cloning the existing cloud Supabase dataset, to serve PSS standalone apps (per docs/SKILL_pss_standalone_app.md). All steps must be documented in docs/ so the user can redeploy themselves. Target VM: 10.0.0.85, Ubuntu 24.04 LTS, 4 vCPU / 8 GB RAM / 50 GB. Apps reach Supabase by LAN IP; not on platform_net (Pi-local Docker bridge).

Runbook docs in this repo MUST avoid backslash-continued multi-line commands in copy-paste blocks. Users (or agents) inevitably paste fragments. Split into independent qm/docker/etc. commands per line, each fully self-contained except for shared shell variables defined inside the same heredoc. The 2026-04-30 'qm create 400 not enough arguments' incident was caused by the user pasting only the qm create block from a backslash-continued sequence, with TEMPLATE_VMID undefined in their shell.

bd create --deps blocks:X creates the WRONG-DIRECTION edge — the new issue ends up blocking X, not blocked by X. After bd create, use 'bd dep add <new-id> <predecessor-id>' or 'bd link <new-id> <predecessor-id>' to set 'new depends on predecessor'. Verified empirically 2026-04-30; had to flip 12 edges.
